focus: Iteration 5 — full per-hook per-plugin port verdict table
noveltyJustification: The consolidated matrix corrects the Claude event-key count, adds the missing PermissionRequest coverage gap, and makes native-import limits explicit for all 19 handlers, 15 plugins, and seven guard cores.

graphEvents.label: Claude settings has seven keys and nineteen handlers
Claude import is a conditional baseline
OpenCode plugin registrations need Devin adapters
Seven guard cores require explicit Devin transport
ADR-001 should make Devin adapters authoritative

label: Claude settings has seven registered event keys, not eight, with nineteen handlers; PermissionRequest is absent
Native Claude import is useful for shell-only lifecycle checks but cannot rewrite matchers, payloads, output envelopes, or lifecycle timing
All fifteen OpenCode plugins cross a host-registration boundary and require selective Devin lifecycle rebuilds or explicit omission
The seven guard cores are reusable policy boundaries, but each requires an explicit Devin transport adapter
Devin-specific matchers and output semantics must remain authoritative in .devin/hooks.v1.json
OpenCode plugin factories are not covered by read_config_from.claude import

direction: Treat native Claude settings import as a complete Devin portability layer
reason: Changed tool names, missing fields, incompatible output envelopes, PreCompact timing, and OpenCode-only registrations leave material behavior uncovered.
